## Content Standards Checklist

### Citations & Evidence
- [ ] All factual claims have citations
- [ ] Statistics include source attribution
- [ ] Quotes are attributed (no hallucinated quotes)
- [ ] Links point to authoritative sources and have been verified

### Voice & Style
- [ ] Uses "we" for recommendations, "you" for direct guidance
- [ ] Professional but accessible tone
- [ ] Key terms in **bold** when first introduced
- [ ] Code/commands in `backticks`

### Structure
- [ ] One main idea per paragraph
- [ ] Real-world examples include specifics (names, dates, impacts)
- [ ] No duplicate coverage of topics already in other chapters

### Incidents & Examples
- [ ] Events actually occurred (not hallucinated)
- [ ] Dates and details are accurate
- [ ] Lessons learned are clearly stated
